AuthorEdge.description:
Same idea per author. We only list authors with ≥ 3 matured posts so
the leaderboard isn't dominated by single lucky calls.
AuthorEdge.title: AuthorEdge

BaselineMetrics.description:
Naive-momentum baseline: rank sectors at each run by their trailing
20d return (no model, no features) and run the same top-3 vs bottom-3
forward-return test. Lets the UI show whether xgb_v1 actually beats
naïve momentum.
BaselineMetrics.title: BaselineMetrics

CatalystScoreBreakdown.description:
Components of the composite catalyst_score so the UI can show *why*
a post scored highly. The stored score is frozen at ingest, so these are
derived from the formula using the post's current hours_old + ticker /
keyword counts; trust is inferred as the residual.
CatalystScoreBreakdown.title: CatalystScoreBreakdown

overall_avg_return_next_day_pct.title: Overall Avg Return Next Day Pct

CategoryTrackRecord.description:
Backtest of a single catalyst category over the lookback window.

Hit rate = fraction of posts with positive +1d return. Avg returns are
in percent. n_with_return excludes posts that haven't seen the next
trading day close yet (very recent posts, weekends).
CategoryTrackRecord.title: CategoryTrackRecord

ChartMarker.description: A point-in-time annotation on the chart. type is what the FE renders.
ChartMarker.title: ChartMarker

run_ts.description: If set, ground the chat on this specific run; otherwise the latest run for the ticker.
run_ts.title: Run Ts

ForwardCallResponse.description:
Structured forward-looking call assembled from the latest run across all
three trained horizons. The frontend renders the prose; this endpoint
supplies the facts.
ForwardCallResponse.title: ForwardCallResponse

HorizonDisagreement.description:
One sector ranked very differently in another horizon's latest run.

`delta = active_rank - other_rank`. Positive delta = the active horizon is
*less bullish* on this symbol than `other_horizon_d`; negative = more bullish.
HorizonDisagreement.title: HorizonDisagreement

ModelPrediction.description:
One row in the latest `reddit_predictions` snapshot.

pred_return_20d is the model's raw expected 20-trading-day return in
percent. pred_score is a 0..100 percentile rank within the snapshot
(anchors the UI badge across model versions / scales). Features is
the raw input vector — useful for debugging / explanation hovers.
ModelPrediction.title: ModelPrediction

PageContext.description:
Snapshot of what the user is currently looking at in the frontend.

Sent on every chat request so the assistant can resolve deictic references
("this ticker", "here", "current sector") without forcing the user to
re-type the symbol that's already on screen.
PageContext.title: PageContext

PredictResponse.description:
Wrapper around the latest model predictions. status='calibrating'
when /reddit_predictions is empty (i.e. not enough matured history
yet to fit the model — see predict_reddit._MIN_TRAIN_EVENTS).
PredictResponse.title: PredictResponse

RuleStats.description:
Backtested win rate + mean 20d forward return for a named pattern.

A row was "matched" on a given (snapshot_date, ticker) if the pattern's
SQL predicate evaluated true for that snapshot. Forward return is the
20-trading-day close-to-close move after the snapshot. n_events is the
count of matched (date, ticker) pairs. Until ≥20 events accumulate the
UI displays this as "calibrating".
RuleStats.title: RuleStats

ScoreComponents.description:
Signed contribution (~ -25..+25 each) of every feature to pred_score.
Sum of these + 50 baseline ≈ pred_score (clamped to 0..100).
ScoreComponents.title: ScoreComponents

ScorecardCalibrationBucket.description:
One row of the calibration ladder: predictions falling in this score
band, and how their realized returns actually came in. If the model is
well-calibrated, mean_realized rises monotonically with score_bucket.
ScorecardCalibrationBucket.title: ScorecardCalibrationBucket

ScorecardCall.description:
One historical prediction with its realized outcome — used for the
top-hits / top-misses lists on the UI panel.
ScorecardCall.title: ScorecardCall

SubredditEdge.description:
Realized predictive value of a subreddit: across every reddit_post that
matured and had a primary_ticker, what's the average realized 20d return
of the ticker after the post? > 0 means posts there have led to upside.
SubredditEdge.title: SubredditEdge

cfp_api__routes__reddit__ScorecardRespon.description:
Production scorekeeping for /reddit/predict and reddit_posts.

status='calibrating' when nothing has matured yet (≤28 calendar days
since the first prediction landed). Once mature, the model section
becomes meaningful; subreddit/author edges populate independently as
catalyst posts age into their 20d window.
cfp_api__routes__reddit__ScorecardRespon.title: ScorecardResponse

info.description: Capital Flow Predictor inference API. Auth via Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY> or X-API-Key header; rate-limited per identity. See docs/API.md for examples.
info.title: Bellwether API

get.description:
Per-agent track record at a given horizon.

Excludes neutral calls from `n_calls` for hit-rate purposes — neutrals
are scored separately (a neutral call hits if |fwd return| < 1%).

Pairwise agreement matrix — for each ordered pair (a, b), what fraction
of (run_ts, ticker) pairs did both agents make the same directional call?
Pairs with agreement > 0.85 indicate one of the two is redundant.

Latest full ensemble (analysts + personas + synthesis) for a ticker.

Pass ``?run_ts=<ISO timestamp>`` to fetch a specific run's signals — useful
for polling a streaming run as agents complete one by one.

parameters.description: If set, return signals for this specific run; otherwise the latest run.
parameters.name: run_ts

schema.description: If set, return signals for this specific run; otherwise the latest run.
schema.title: Run Ts

get.description:
OHLCV bars + chart markers (flow alerts, insider txns, earnings).

Reads from prices_daily + uw_flow_alerts + uw_insider_transactions +
uw_earnings. Flow alerts are filtered to >$1M premium so the chart
doesn't fill with noise.

Compare two personas' latest takes on the same ticker.

Reads the latest agent_signals row per (ticker, persona) — these may come
from different runs, which is intentional: a persona that hasn't fired
recently still appears with its last known stance.

post.description:
Kick off a new ensemble run for `ticker`. Returns immediately with a run_ts.

The actual run takes ~30-40 seconds. Poll
``GET /v1/agents/{ticker}/runs/{run_ts}`` to watch agents complete one by one.

When ``provider`` is supplied (Deep Analysis button), every LLM call in the
run is routed to that provider instead of the process default. For
``provider=anthropic``, ``tier`` selects the model.

parameters.description: Override LLM provider for this run (e.g. 'anthropic'). Empty = process default.
parameters.name: provider

schema.description: Override LLM provider for this run (e.g. 'anthropic'). Empty = process default.
schema.title: Provider

parameters.description: Deep Analysis tier when provider=anthropic: 'sonnet' or 'opus'.
parameters.name: tier

schema.description: Deep Analysis tier when provider=anthropic: 'sonnet' or 'opus'.
schema.title: Tier

get.description:
Polling endpoint for a specific run.

Returns ``is_complete=True`` when the Portfolio Manager signal has landed,
which is the last node in the ensemble graph.
Historical signals for one (ticker, agent) pair, newest first.

parameters.description: Agent name, e.g. portfolio_manager, buffett, technicals
parameters.name: agent

schema.description: Agent name, e.g. portfolio_manager, buffett, technicals
schema.title: Agent

post.description: SSE stream: text tokens + tool_call events + tool_result events + done.

get.description: Return the bootstrap percentile distribution of return/DD/Sharpe/win-rate.

schema.title: Response Monte Carlo V1 Backtest Monte Carlo Get

get.description:
Top tickers right now where the flow is loud, opening, aggressive, and
corroborated by other smart-money signals. Reads the heuristic 0..100
`whale_conviction_signals` table refreshed every ~5min by the scorer.
Per-table freshness + row counts. Returns 200 always; clients inspect ``status_per_table``.

schema.title: Response Detailed Health V1 Health Detailed Get

parameters.description: Anchor date for the rolling window (default = today)
parameters.name: as_of

schema.description: Anchor date for the rolling window (default = today)
schema.title: As Of

parameters.description: Add macro tickers (VIX, TLT, dollar, gold, oil, HY, BTC) as nodes
parameters.name: include_macros

schema.description: Add macro tickers (VIX, TLT, dollar, gold, oil, HY, BTC) as nodes
schema.title: Include Macros

get.description: Directed lead → follower edges from the latest Granger computation.

parameters.description: XGB horizon used to color nodes (5, 10, 20)
parameters.name: horizon

schema.description: XGB horizon used to color nodes (5, 10, 20)
schema.title: Horizon

get.description:
For each symbol whose latest daily return exceeds `sigma` of its 30d
distribution, return the historical Granger followers from the latest
lead_lag_matrix snapshot. Surfaces the user's "DELL moved → watch IREN"
signal: an unusual move in a leader is a heads-up that its followers may
move next.

parameters.description: Z-score threshold for an unusual move
parameters.name: sigma

schema.description: Z-score threshold for an unusual move
schema.title: Sigma

parameters.description: Max Granger p-value for follower edges
parameters.name: max_p

schema.description: Max Granger p-value for follower edges
schema.title: Max P

get.description: Top constituents of an ETF, tethered to the parent and connected by correlation.

parameters.description: Top constituents by weight to include
parameters.name: top

schema.description: Top constituents by weight to include
schema.title: Top

get.description:
Most recent predictions for the given horizon + model, sorted by rank.

Re-ranks by `score` on the read side to defend against the historically
degenerate `predictions.rank` column (same workaround pattern as /v1/network).

Standalone endpoint for the same backtest stats so the front-end can
lazy-load it without slowing the main /mentions request.

schema.title: Response Get Backtest V1 Reddit Backtest Get

get.description:
Per-category hit rate and average next-trading-day return for
catalyst-flagged Reddit posts. Lets the UI show whether the signal has
measurable edge before users trust the live feed.

Catalyst-flagged Reddit posts ordered by composite score, enriched
with engagement (upvotes/comments), score breakdown, and lead-ticker
price reaction (daily granularity).

parameters.name: ticker

schema.title: Ticker

parameters.description: Filter to tickers in this watchlist sector
parameters.name: sector

schema.title: Sector

parameters.description: Restrict to tickers in the latest watchlists run
parameters.name: watchlist

schema.title: Watchlist

parameters.description: Include 5d forward-return aggregates by spike bucket
parameters.name: backtest

schema.description: Include 5d forward-return aggregates by spike bucket
schema.title: Backtest

get.description:
Return the latest ML predictions (one row per ticker for the most
recent snapshot). Calibrating until the model has enough matured
history to train responsibly.

Backtested historical edge for every predictive rule. Used by the UI
to show how much past evidence backs each matched-rule chip on a row.

schema.title: Response Get Rule Stats V1 Reddit Rules Get

get.description:
How well has the predictor + the reddit feed actually called the
market? Looks at every prediction / catalyst post with a matured 20d
return inside [today - window_days, today] and computes hit rates,
mean error, calibration, and a leaderboard of best/worst calls plus
per-subreddit + per-author predictive edge.
Sectors with their latest rank, prior rank, confidence, and a rank-history sparkline.

parameters.description: How many recent runs to include in rank_history.
parameters.name: history

schema.description: How many recent runs to include in rank_history.
schema.title: History

get.description:
Forward-looking call assembled from the latest run of the chosen horizon
plus the latest runs of the other trained horizons, so the UI can flag
cross-horizon disagreement and rank stability.

Relative Rotation Graph for sector + theme ETFs against the benchmark.

Uses the JdK-style construction: take the price ratio sector/benchmark,
z-score it over a rolling n_window (default ~3 months) and re-center to 100
to get RS-Ratio. Z-score that series over the same window to get RS-Momentum.
The 4-quadrant plot lets traders see at a glance which sectors are
*currently* outperforming AND accelerating (leading) vs. fading from a
prior lead (weakening) vs. underperforming but turning up (improving).

Daily bars; tail_weeks * 5 trading days of trail per sector.

Backtest the model: for each of the last N runs, compare the actual
forward-`horizon` return of the top-3 ranked sectors vs the bottom-3,
compute the Spearman rank IC between model rank and realized return,
and run a naïve 20d-momentum baseline over the same runs.

A "hit" = top-3 basket avg return > bottom-3 basket avg return over horizon.
Skips runs where the horizon hasn't elapsed yet (no realized data).
Full constituent list for `etf` with per-name returns + options sentiment + model score.
List Finviz screener presets the frontend can offer as universe filters.
Rank stocks the agents have analyzed by options-trade attractiveness.

schema.title: Finviz Preset

get.description:
Recently-analyzed tickers grouped by sector.

Source of truth is `agent_signals` (every ticker the ensemble has run on).
Curated `watchlists` rows enrich rank / target_weight / sector when available;
otherwise we derive sector from `sector_holdings` and rank within sector by
PM confidence.
One sector's recently-analyzed tickers (same merge as the top-level route).
